speaker: LUCENTIO
text: At last, though long, our jarring notes agree,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And time it is when raging war is done

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: To smile at ’scapes and perils overblown.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: My fair Bianca, bid my father welcome,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: While I with selfsame kindness welcome thine.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Brother Petruchio, sister Katherina,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And thou, Hortensio, with thy loving widow,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Feast with the best, and welcome to my house.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: My banquet is to close our stomachs up

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: After our great good cheer. Pray you, sit down,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: For now we sit to chat as well as eat.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Nothing but sit and sit, and eat and eat!

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Padua affords this kindness, son Petruchio.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Padua affords nothing but what is kind.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: For both our sakes I would that word were true.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Now, for my life, Hortensio fears his widow!

speaker: WIDOW
text: Then never trust me if I be afeard.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: You are very sensible, and yet you miss my sense:

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I mean Hortensio is afeard of you.

speaker: WIDOW
text: He that is giddy thinks the world turns round.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Roundly replied.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Mistress, how mean you that?

speaker: WIDOW
text: Thus I conceive by him.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Conceives by me? How likes Hortensio that?

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: My widow says, thus she conceives her tale.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Very well mended. Kiss him for that, good widow.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: “He that is giddy thinks the world turns round”—

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I pray you tell me what you meant by that.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Your husband being troubled with a shrew

speaker: WIDOW
text: Measures my husband’s sorrow by his woe.

speaker: WIDOW
text: And now you know my meaning.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: A very mean meaning.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Right, I mean you.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And I am mean indeed, respecting you.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: To her, Kate!

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: To her, widow!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: A hundred marks, my Kate does put her down.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: That’s my office.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Spoke like an officer! Ha’ to thee, lad.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: How likes Gremio these quick-witted folks?

speaker: GREMIO
text: Believe me, sir, they butt together well.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Head and butt! An hasty-witted body

speaker: BIANCA
text: Would say your head and butt were head and horn.

speaker: VINCENTIO
text: Ay, mistress bride, hath that awakened you?

speaker: BIANCA
text: Ay, but not frighted me. Therefore I’ll sleep again.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Nay, that you shall not. Since you have begun,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Have at you for a bitter jest or two.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Am I your bird? I mean to shift my bush,

speaker: BIANCA
text: And then pursue me as you draw your bow.—

speaker: BIANCA
text: You are welcome all.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: She hath prevented me. Here, Signior Tranio,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: This bird you aimed at, though you hit her not.—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Therefore a health to all that shot and missed.

speaker: TRANIO
text: O, sir, Lucentio slipped me like his greyhound,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Which runs himself and catches for his master.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: A good swift simile, but something currish.

speaker: TRANIO
text: ’Tis well, sir, that you hunted for yourself.

speaker: TRANIO
text: ’Tis thought your deer does hold you at a bay.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: O, O, Petruchio! Tranio hits you now.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: I thank thee for that gird, good Tranio.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Confess, confess! Hath he not hit you here?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: He has a little galled me, I confess.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And as the jest did glance away from me,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: ’Tis ten to one it maimed you two outright.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Now, in good sadness, son Petruchio,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: I think thou hast the veriest shrew of all.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Well, I say no. And therefore, for assurance,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Let’s each one send unto his wife,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And he whose wife is most obedient

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: To come at first when he doth send for her

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Shall win the wager which we will propose.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Content, what’s the wager?

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Twenty crowns.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Twenty crowns?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I’ll venture so much of my hawk or hound,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: But twenty times so much upon my wife.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: A hundred, then.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Content.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: A match! ’Tis done.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Who shall begin?

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: That will I.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Go, Biondello, bid your mistress come to me.

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: I go.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Son, I’ll be your half Bianca comes.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: I’ll have no halves. I’ll bear it all myself.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: How now, what news?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Sir, my mistress sends you word

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: That she is busy, and she cannot come.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: How? “She’s busy, and she cannot come”?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Is that an answer?

speaker: GREMIO
text: Ay, and a kind one, too.

speaker: GREMIO
text: Pray God, sir, your wife send you not a worse.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I hope better.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Sirrah Biondello, go and entreat my wife

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: To come to me forthwith.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: O ho, entreat her!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Nay, then, she must needs come.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: I am afraid, sir,

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Do what you can, yours will not be entreated.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Now, where’s my wife?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: She says you have some goodly jest in hand.

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: She will not come. She bids you come to her.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Worse and worse. She will not come!

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: O vile, intolerable, not to be endured!—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Sirrah Grumio, go to your mistress,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Say I command her come to me.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: I know her answer.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: What?

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: She will not.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: The fouler fortune mine, and there an end.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Now by my holidam, here comes Katherina!

speaker: KATHERINE
text: What is your will, sir, that you send for me?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Where is your sister, and Hortensio’s wife?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: They sit conferring by the parlor fire.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Go fetch them hither. If they deny to come,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Swinge me them soundly forth unto their husbands.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Away, I say, and bring them hither straight.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Here is a wonder, if you talk of a wonder.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: And so it is. I wonder what it bodes.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Marry, peace it bodes, and love, and quiet life,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: An awful rule, and right supremacy,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And, to be short, what not that’s sweet and happy.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Now fair befall thee, good Petruchio!

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: The wager thou hast won, and I will add

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Unto their losses twenty thousand crowns,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Another dowry to another daughter,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: For she is changed as she had never been.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Nay, I will win my wager better yet,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And show more sign of her obedience,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Her new-built virtue and obedience.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: See where she comes, and brings your froward wives

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: As prisoners to her womanly persuasion.—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Katherine, that cap of yours becomes you not.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Off with that bauble, throw it underfoot.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Lord, let me never have a cause to sigh

speaker: WIDOW
text: Till I be brought to such a silly pass.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Fie, what a foolish duty call you this?

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: I would your duty were as foolish too.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: The wisdom of your duty, fair Bianca,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Hath cost me a hundred crowns since suppertime.

speaker: BIANCA
text: The more fool you for laying on my duty.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Katherine, I charge thee tell these headstrong women

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: What duty they do owe their lords and husbands.

speaker: WIDOW
text: Come, come, you’re mocking. We will have no telling.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Come on, I say, and first begin with her.

speaker: WIDOW
text: She shall not.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I say she shall.—And first begin with her.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Fie, fie! Unknit that threat’ning unkind brow,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And dart not scornful glances from those eyes

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To wound thy lord, thy king, thy governor.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: It blots thy beauty as frosts do bite the meads,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Confounds thy fame as whirlwinds shake fair buds,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And in no sense is meet or amiable.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: A woman moved is like a fountain troubled,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Muddy, ill-seeming, thick, bereft of beauty,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And while it is so, none so dry or thirsty

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Will deign to sip or touch one drop of it.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Thy husband is thy lord, thy life, thy keeper,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Thy head, thy sovereign, one that cares for thee,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And for thy maintenance commits his body

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To painful labor both by sea and land,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To watch the night in storms, the day in cold,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Whilst thou liest warm at home, secure and safe,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And craves no other tribute at thy hands

speaker: KATHERINE
text: But love, fair looks, and true obedience—

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Too little payment for so great a debt.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Such duty as the subject owes the prince,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Even such a woman oweth to her husband;

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And when she is froward, peevish, sullen, sour,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And not obedient to his honest will,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: What is she but a foul contending rebel

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And graceless traitor to her loving lord?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I am ashamed that women are so simple

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To offer war where they should kneel for peace,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Or seek for rule, supremacy, and sway

speaker: KATHERINE
text: When they are bound to serve, love, and obey.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Why are our bodies soft and weak and smooth,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Unapt to toil and trouble in the world,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: But that our soft conditions and our hearts

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Should well agree with our external parts?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Come, come, you froward and unable worms!

speaker: KATHERINE
text: My mind hath been as big as one of yours,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: My heart as great, my reason haply more,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To bandy word for word and frown for frown;

speaker: KATHERINE
text: But now I see our lances are but straws,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Our strength as weak, our weakness past compare,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: That seeming to be most which we indeed least are.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Then vail your stomachs, for it is no boot,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And place your hands below your husband’s foot;

speaker: KATHERINE
text: In token of which duty, if he please,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: My hand is ready, may it do him ease.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Why, there’s a wench! Come on, and kiss me, Kate.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Well, go thy ways, old lad, for thou shalt ha ’t.

speaker: VINCENTIO
text: ’Tis a good hearing when children are toward.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: But a harsh hearing when women are froward.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Come, Kate, we’ll to bed.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: We three are married, but you two are sped.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text:
To
                 
                Lucentio
                .
                 
              ’Twas I won the wager, though you hit the white,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And being a winner, God give you good night.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Now, go thy ways, thou hast tamed a curst shrow.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: ’Tis a wonder, by your leave, she will be tamed so.
